response: The internet originated in 1960s U.S. Defense Department networking research and went public in 1969 when ARPANET linked UCLA to Stanford; its modern architecture solidified with the 1983 adoption of TCP/IP. Key milestones followed: Tim Berners-Lee’s 1989 creation of the World Wide Web (HTML, URLs, HTTP), the 1993 Mosaic browser that opened the web to mainstream users, the late-1990s dot-com boom and 2001 crash that still left giants like Amazon and Google standing, and the mid-2000s rise of social media platforms such as Facebook, Twitter, and YouTube. Mobile and cloud revolutions—sparked by the 2007 iPhone and AWS’s 2006 debut—made internet access ubiquitous and computing on-demand. Today the network connects over 5 billion people and supports nearly every sphere of commerce, communication, and entertainment.
